Igal Talianski.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): Visual artist from Haifa, Israel (also referenced as Igalski). Collage-focused practice using recycled materials; also branding/marketing work for climbing-sector clients (e.g., Boulder Haifa, Seven Bays Bouldering per shop bio).
Igal Talianski.exhibitionsText:
2022 — Group, Illustration Edition 2022 (LaCulture), Tel Aviv area, Israel (200 illustrators; verify venue copy)
2019 — Group, Re-purposing: Old books take on a new life, Edmond de Rothschild Center, Tel Aviv, Israel (curated Galit Gaon)
Igal Talianski.pressText:
EDR Center — 2019 — Re-purposing exhibition page — https://edrcenter.com/en/exhibitions/re-purposing-old-books-take-on-a-new-life/
LaCulture TLV — 2022 — Illustration Edition 2022 — https://laculturetlv.com/exhibition/illustration-edition-2022/

Carsten Gueth.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): Visual artist based in Stuttgart, Germany; architecture/urban planning training; long career in graphic design for cultural institutions and agencies. Recent awards cited in profiles include Creative Länd Award 2024, ICMA 2024 Gold, ADC 2025, Red Dot 2025 (verify on primary sources). Also publishes as / collaborates under music-adjacent project context (notimefortv.biz / Die Doing—confirm naming).
Carsten Gueth.exhibitionsText:
2025 — Solo, Rituals for the Unknown, Palau de la Música Catalana, Barcelona, Spain
2025 — Solo, Klang & Krach, Literaturhaus Stuttgart, Stuttgart, Germany
2024 — Solo, Klang & Krach, Silbersalzfestival, Halle, Germany
2022 — Group, 2022% OFF, Jupiter Hamburg, Hamburg, Germany
Carsten Gueth.pressText:
Palau de la Música Catalana — 2025 — Rituals for the Unknown exhibition — https://www.palaumusica.cat/en/exhibition-rituals-for-the-unknown-by-carsten-gueth_1514921
AATONAU — — Studio / practice feature — https://aatonau.com/carsten-guth-a-dance-of-color-and-contrast/
Artinrug — — Artist profile (Die Doing) — https://artinrug.com/artist/die-doing-carsten-gueth/

Or Bar El.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): Or Bar-El—illustrator, animator, and video editor in Israel. Practice spans digital illustration, street murals combining graphic and painterly approaches; inspiration from everyday street photography (third-party profile on drorhadadi.com). Do not conflate with Ido Bar-El (b. 1959, different practice). Confirm education and exhibition list on orbarel.com and primary interviews.
Or Bar El.pressText: Dror Hadadi — — Street art / mural feature (Hebrew) — https://drorhadadi.com/%D7%90%D7%95%D7%A8-%D7%91%D7%A8-%D7%90%D7%9C-%D7%A1%D7%99%D7%98%D7%95%D7%90%D7%A6%D7%99%D7%95%D7%AA-%D7%99%D7%95%D7%9E%D7%99%D7%95%D7%9E%D7%99%D7%95%D7%AA/

My Sunbeam.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): London-based illustrator working as My Sunbeam (profile lists first name Taylor; verify legal name on contracts). People of Print member; playful characters with distressed/retro textures and detailed linework. Prints via GoodMood Prints, East Side Studio London, etc.
My Sunbeam.pressText:
People of Print — — POP member profile — https://members.peopleofprint.com/profile/my-sunbeam/
GoodMood Prints — — Artist collection — https://www.goodmoodprints.com/artists/mysunbeam

Linda Baritski.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): Linda Baritski (SEASONOFVICTORY), London—illustrator, designer, art director; freelance since 2017. Represented by IllustrationX. Major project: Kiehl’s global campaign 2022–23 across 48 locations (Creative Boom, World Illustration Awards). Clients cited include Cancer Research UK, Harrods, Vita Coco, Pacifico, and others (verify on portfolio).
Linda Baritski.pressText:
Creative Boom — 2023 — Kiehl’s global campaign interview — https://www.creativeboom.com/inspiration/illustrator-linda-baritski-on-the-challenges-of-working-on-a-global-branding-project/
The AOI / WIA — 2022 — World Illustration Awards entry — https://theaoi.com/wia/linda-baritski-aka-seasonofvictory-kiehls-loves-global-campaign-2022-23/

Erezoo.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): Erez Sameach (Erezoo), Haifa-based illustrator, graphic designer, and street artist; from Carmiel; Visual Communication graduate, Wizo Haifa. Co-founded SIRA collaborative space. Social art projects (Pop Up Museum, Street Crafting, BONA!, etc.—see erezoo.com). Brother Ziv Ink (street artist); practices permission-forward street work per interview summaries.
Erezoo.exhibitionsText: 2023 — Group, street art exhibition context, Negev Museum of Art, Beersheba, Israel (verify official title)
